name: 关系与配对视角
description: 用于把研究对象从个人层面推进到夫妻、伴侣、家庭成员或其他配对关系中的相互影响。

clusters.meaning: 从个人层面转向配对/夫妻层面的研究视角

expressions.note: 比一般的 using theory 更能说明家庭成员经历相互嵌入，适合夫妻或家庭过程研究。
expressions.example: Drawing on a linked-lives perspective, we examine couples' decisions.

expressions.note: 把 longitudinal、couple-level 和 dyadic 三个限定压缩到一个高密度方法名词组。
expressions.example: We use longitudinal couple-level dyadic data from the UK.

expressions.note: 强调不平等发生在配对内部，而不是群体均值差异。
expressions.example: The models estimate changes in within-couple inequality.

expressions.en: By going beyond an individual-centred focus, this study reveals...
expressions.note: 适合把方法/理论贡献写成视角转移，而不是简单说 we use dyadic data。
expressions.example: By going beyond an individual-centred focus, this study reveals dyadic dynamics.

expressions.en: shift the unit of explanation from individuals to couples
expressions.note: 把“为什么用 dyadic data”变成论证动作，避免只报数据结构。
expressions.example: We shift the unit of explanation from individuals to couples.

clusters.meaning: 关系内部的互依、交叉影响和共同塑造

expressions.note: 比 influence 更具体，强调一方特征对另一方结果的跨人效应。
expressions.example: The analysis considers crossover effects between spouses.

expressions.note: 比 affect together 更简洁，适合多个主体或机制共同作用。
expressions.example: Both spouses' ideologies jointly shape housework time.

expressions.note: 比 interaction 更书面，适合理论段和讨论段描述复杂关系。
expressions.example: We examine the interplay of norms and resources.

expressions.note: 语气较强，适合讨论段总结复杂配对关系，不宜用于简单相关。
expressions.example: The findings reveal intricate interdependencies within couples.
